element_introduction_order.element: Grab mechanics (body slam, pile driver, atomic backdrop)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Advanced grappling options available from start

element_introduction_order.element: First opponent monster (Geon, Rocky, Beetle Mania, or Astro Guy)
Six-monster cycle (player fights all six opponents sequentially)
Self-encounter (fighting palette-swapped version of chosen monster)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: End of first cycle (Round 6 final opponent)

element_introduction_order.element: City variation (same monsters in different environments)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady escalation with cyclical reset. First cycle establishes opponent patterns; second cycle resets tension through environmental novelty while maintaining mechanical difficulty. Total 12 rounds (8 in 16-bit ports) creates moderate-length session structure.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate. First three opponents establish basic combat loop and player's monster moveset. Punch/kick/grab mechanics available from start reduce skill floor.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate to challenging. Opponents 4-6 in first cycle introduce pattern recognition demands. Self-encounter at cycle end acts as skill check.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Second cycle (Rounds 7-12/8) reuses opponent patterns in new cities, reducing novelty but allowing mastery through repetition. Same mechanical difficulty as early game with psychological shift from environment change.
notable_spikes: Self-encounter (Round 6/first-cycle finale): Opponent mirrors player's chosen monster and fighting style
Transition to second cycle: Psychological tension from visual novelty despite same opponent lineup

notable_level_patterns.description: Fixed sequence of 6 playable monsters as opponents (Geon, Rocky, Beetle Mania, Astro Guy, Woo/Poison Ghost in arcade). Player fights all six in deterministic order, then repeats cycle in different cities.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tokyo Round 1 vs Geon → Round 2 vs next opponent in sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: First cycle concludes with player fighting palette-swapped clone of their chosen monster. Creates psychological difficulty peak by forcing player to face their own combat patterns.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Round 6 (end of first cycle): Chosen monster palette-swapped as final opponent

notable_level_patterns.description: Same 6-monster lineup repeats in different Japanese cities across second cycle (Tokyo, Kyoto, Okayama, Osaka, Kobe, Hiroshima in arcade; SNES omits Kobe/Hiroshima). Environment changes but opponent patterns remain identical.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Rounds 7-12: Same opponent sequence in new city backdrops

notable_level_patterns.description: Skyscrapers and stadiums can be destroyed during combat. Environmental interaction mechanic available throughout all rounds.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any round: Building destruction during grapple exchanges
